edition.dagbeeld: De NAVO-top in Ankara domineert het nieuws: Trump flirt met F-35-verkoop aan Turkije, Rutte slijmt strategisch, en Europa bouwt haastig aan defensie zonder samenhang. Tegelijk blaast Iran — via een aanslag op een tanker in de Straat van Hormuz — de spanningen opnieuw op, en laat Macrons bezoek aan Damascus zien hoe kwetsbaar de nieuwe Syrische orde nog is.

article.title: Trump’s NATO-bashing casts shadow over U.S. pledge to defend allies
article.summary: NATO’s Article 5 has long promised that an attack against one is an attack against all. President Donald Trump is not so sure.

opening.note: Trump zaait twijfel over Article 5 terwijl NAVO-top in volle gang is.
opening.label: analyse

article.title: Tanker set on fire after being struck in Strait of Hormuz, British military says
article.summary: The ship was struck with a projectile near Oman, a British maritime monitor said. No casualties were reported and Iran and the U.S. did not immediately comment.

front.note: Nieuwe aanval op tanker in Hormuz zet wapenstilstand VS-Iran onder druk.
front.label: breaking

article.summary: Het Tulp Fonds werd in het leven geroepen na berichten over de bedreiging van de academische vrijheid in de Verenigde Staten. Het wil internationale toponderzoekers naar Nederland halen.

article.title: Türkiye stifles dissent ahead of NATO meeting
article.summary: More than 200 people were arrested in protest marches and anti-terror raids, including academics, lawyers, and journalists.

article.title: UN warns of ‘catastrophe’ unfolding in Sudan
article.summary: The head of the Human Rights Council said the besieged town has been subject to “relentless” drone strikes, executions, torture, and sexual violence.

items.title: Why China Fired a Long-Range Missile Into the Pacific
items.summary: Beijing had long tested nuclear missiles only within its borders. A rare launch over an ocean shows it is determined to narrow the gap with the United States.

items.title: A quiet shift in how Americans perceive local news
items.summary: Local news has long been an exception to a troubling trend. Even as trust in national news organizations eroded over the past decade and partisan divides over the media widened, local news largely held its ground — majorities of both Republicans and Democrats continued to rate their local news media favorably.
